task_description: Assist Logan with his dental hygiene routine to ensure proper care of teeth and to ease his anxiety related to dental visits.
Conduct regular reviews of the Education, Health and Care Plan (ECHP) to assess Logan’s progress and any support required.
Discuss strategies to support Logan in reducing nicotine usage from vaping and promote healthier habits.
Monitor and document any new skin reactions that Logan experiences while awaiting his dermatology referral.
Establish a sleep hygiene routine to improve Logan's sleep quality and overall mental health.
Assist Logan with personal care tasks like washing and brushing teeth to promote hygiene and independence.

description: Assess Logan’s adherence to prescribed medication, including Zolmitriptan for migraines, and provide reminders.
instructions: 1. Review current medications with Logan. 2. Discuss the importance of taking Zolmitriptan as prescribed. 3. Set reminders for medication times. 4. If he refuses to take medication, explore reasons and discuss alternatives or adjustments with his healthcare team.

description: Check and ensure Logan is up to date with his immunizations and arrange for any necessary vaccinations.
instructions: 1. Review Logan's immunization records. 2. Identify any missing immunizations based on age and medical guidance. 3. Contact healthcare provider to schedule any necessary appointments. 4. Remind Logan about the importance of staying vaccinated for his health.

description: Engage with Logan in an emotional support session to discuss feelings and challenges he is facing, especially regarding ADHD and school attendance.
instructions: 1. Find a quiet, comfortable space free from distractions. 2. Initiate a conversation by asking open-ended questions about how he has been feeling. 3. Actively listen and provide empathetic responses. 4. Encourage him to express his thoughts and feelings freely. 5. Summarize the key points discussed and ask if there's anything else he wants to talk about.

description: Support Logan in preparing healthy meals consistent with a balanced diet to promote overall health and well-being.
instructions: 1. Plan meals for the day ensuring they are balanced (vegetables, protein, grains). 2. Assist Logan in choosing snacks that are healthy and align with dietary restrictions. 3. Prepare the meals together, teaching him cooking skills along the way. 4. Discuss the importance of maintaining good nutrition for his health.

description: Provide a safe space for Logan to express his feelings related to mental health, discussing any substance misuse concerns.
instructions: 1. Find a quiet environment for discussion. 2. Ask open questions about his mental health and any struggles he faces, especially regarding substance use. 3. Listen and validate his feelings without judgement. 4. Provide resources or referrals for additional support if necessary.

description: Encourage Logan to engage in regular physical activities suited to his interests to promote fitness and health.
instructions: 1. Discuss preferred physical activities (sports, walks, etc.) with Logan. 2. Schedule daily activity time based on his interests. 3. Participate with him in these activities to motivate and support him. 4. Monitor his response and enjoyment to stay engaged.

description: Monitor Logan's school attendance and provide support or solutions to improve attendance.
instructions: 1. Review Logan's school attendance records. 2. Discuss any barriers he faces in attending school. 3. Collaborate to create a plan for overcoming these barriers, setting realistic goals for attendance. 4. Stay in contact with school personnel if needed.
Assist Logan in choosing activities; ensure he remains hydrated.
Use visual aids, toothpaste that he prefers, ensure comfort.

task_name: Meal planning with nutrients focusing on balanced diet
instructions: Include variety of foods, avoided allergens like cheese.
Ensure Logan feels comfortable and safe to express feelings.

Risk monitoring for Absconding/Missing From Care (MFC). Multiple incidents of MFC, including leaving home with peers for inappropriate activities (e.g., substance use, festivals, unsafe locations). Threats to walk out of the home and to local bridge. Control measures: Team vigilance, Door sensors (previously installed), Consistent boundaries and routines, De-escalation strategies when unsettled, Engaging Logan in positive activities

Risk monitoring for Substance Misuse. Disclosed use of ketamine and other illegal substances. Has been involved in gang-related activities and potential sexual exploitation risks. History of Missing From Care (MFC) linked to substance misuse. Control measures: Completion of YPDAAT course, CAMHS discussions, Exploration of EMDR & CBT therapy, Team vigilance for absconding, Consistent boundaries and routines, Functional assessments of behaviours

Risk monitoring for Self-harm and Suicide Ideation. History of superficial self-harm and suicide ideations. Expresses feelings of distress with phrases like 'I really don't like cheesecakes' or 'I want to make a cheesecake'. Control measures: Consistent de-escalation techniques, Key work sessions, CAMHS involvement, Barnardo's sessions, Exploring EMDR & CBT therapy, 1:1 talks, Offering time/space away from others, Referring to Self-Help and Ask a Question facilities, Contacting CAMHS Duty Professional/Crisis Line, Contacting GP/Social Care/999/A&E if unable to keep safe

Remind and assist Mrs. Vance to drink sufficient fluids throughout the day.
instructions: 1. Schedule times for fluid intake reminders (e.g., every hour). 2. Provide visual cues like a water bottle or cup nearby. 3. Encourage her to drink water or other fluids, checking that she has consumed enough during the visit. 4. Document her fluid intake amounts. 5. Follow up on her hydration status at subsequent visits.

description: Provide companionship and engage in social activities like reading or listening to music.
instructions: 1. Spend time with Mrs. Vance during the visit to reduce feelings of isolation. 2. Ask her about her favorite books or music and engage in those activities. 3. Encourage sharing memories or stories to foster a connection. 4. Note her mood and engagement level to report any significant changes.

description: Reposition Mrs. Vance regularly to reduce the risk of pressure sores due to limited mobility.
instructions: 1. Assess Mrs. Vance's current position and overall comfort. 2. Gently reposition her every 2 hours during the visit to relieve pressure. 3. Discuss proper body mechanics with her for future reference. 4. Check for any areas of redness or irritation and document them. 5. Educate her on the importance of repositioning even when alone.

description: Assist Mrs. Vance with her nighttime routine and commode needs during evening hours.
instructions: 1. Visit Mrs. Vance in the early evening to assist with final preparations for bed. 2. Remind her of her medication if it's due. 3. Help her with any nighttime snacks or fluids if needed. 4. Assist her when she needs to wake for the commode, ensuring she is safe during night movements. 5. Discuss any nighttime confusion and ensure she is calm before sleep.

description: Administer and monitor prescribed medication.
instructions: 1. Confirm with Mrs. Vance the medications to be taken at this time. 2. Gather the medications and ensure they are correct as per the medication chart. 3. Administer medications, monitoring her for any immediate side effects. 4. Document the administration in the medication log. 5. Set reminders for the next scheduled medication times to prevent missed doses.

description: Provide assistance with washing, dressing, and grooming tasks.
instructions: 1. Gather necessary personal care items (soap, shampoo, towels, clothes). 2. Assist Mrs. Vance in the bathroom by providing support while she bathes. 3. Offer physical assistance as needed for dressing, ensuring comfort and modesty. 4. Help with grooming tasks like brushing hair and applying lotion if necessary. 5. Encourage her to do as much as she can independently for her dignity and confidence.

description: Assist Mrs. Vance with toileting needs, particularly during night hours.
instructions: 1. Be attentive to signs of discomfort or requests to use the commode. 2. Assist her to the toilet when needed and ensure she is safely seated. 3. Provide assistance with changing into fresh incontinence pants if needed afterward. 4. Document each occurrence for tracking. 5. Regularly check for changes in her continence pattern to review with healthcare team.

description: Assist Mrs. Vance with transfers and moving around safely due to unsteady gait.
instructions: 1. Approach Mrs. Vance calmly and confirm she needs assistance. 2. Use gait belt or supportive grip to help her up. 3. Assist her to stand, checking balance before moving. 4. Guide her slowly to desired location, ensuring the path is clear. 5. Provide physical support as necessary and encourage safe movement.

description: Help John with wound inspection and night grooming tasks.
instructions: 1. Gather evening personal care items (wound care supplies, face wash, moisturizer). 2. Assist John in a comfortable position. 3. Inspect catheter site and clean if necessary. 4. Help him wash his face and apply moisturizer if desired. 5. Ensure he feels ready for the night and offer support as needed.

description: Assist John with transferring from wheelchair to bed or other surfaces safely.
instructions: 1. Communicate with John about when he needs assistance. 2. Use a gait belt if understood and needed. 3. Ensure wheelchair brakes are locked. 4. Position yourself on his strong side. 5. With John's help, pivot him to the desired surface while maintaining stability and safety. 6. Help him get comfortable once settled.

description: Perform catheter care including cleaning and checking for kinks or blockages.
instructions: 1. Gather catheter care supplies (gloves, antiseptic wipes, scissors if needed, and clean container). 2. Explain the process to John. 3. Wear gloves to protect against latex allergies. 4. Check the catheter for any kinks, blockages, or signs of infection. 5. Clean the catheter insertion site with antiseptic wipes. 6. Ensure the drainage bag is positioned correctly and not causing discomfort. 7. Document any concerns or changes in John’s condition.

description: Assist John with personal grooming including brushing teeth, washing face, and combing hair.
instructions: 1. Gather all necessary personal care items (toothbrush, toothpaste, face wash, towel, hairbrush). 2. Make sure to wear gloves to avoid contact with latex. 3. Assist John in a comfortable position. 4. Help him brush his teeth and wash his face. 5. Use a hairbrush to comb his hair. 6. Ensure John feels comfortable and respected during the process.
